title: Support for 'unique if'
body:
---


Author Name: **Jeff Bush** (@jbush001)
Original Redmine Issue: 725 from https://www.veripool.org
Original Date: 2014-03-16
Original Assignee: Jeff Bush (@jbush001)

---

The following patch adds support for validating unique and unique0 constraints for a series of 'if...else...if' statements.  Specifically:
- If unique or unique0 are specified, then no more than one of the if statements should evaluate to true
- if there is not a fall-through default else clause and unique is specified then at least one of the statements must be true or an assert will be triggered (per the system verilog spec v3.1a, section 8.4)
- If unique0 is specified, then it is not an error if none of the if statements evalutes to true (this appears to be non-standard behavior, but I made it consistent with the way 'case' works)

I validated this by creating a test program that manually walked through negative and positive cases for the scenarios described above. This is my first time in this code base, so feel free to scrutinize the patch; there may be subtle semantics I missed.
